hawkish_phrases: the risk of cutting policy rates too early was still seen as outweighing that of cutting rates too late
it was premature to discuss rate cuts at the present meeting
Further progress needed to be made in the disinflationary process
domestic inflation remained high at 4.5%, owing to the elevated pace of wage increases
dovish_phrases: disinflation was broadly proceeding as envisaged, or even at a slightly faster pace than previously expected
the new staff projections due in March were likely to show a downward revision to inflation for 2024
Almost all measures of underlying inflation had declined further in December
measures of shorter-term inflation expectations had come down markedly
the incoming information had broadly confirmed the Governing Council's previous assessment of the medium-term inflation outlook
summary: The ECB Governing Council unanimously held rates unchanged with explicit messaging that it was premature to discuss cuts despite faster-than-expected disinflation, maintaining a cautious bias that cutting too early posed greater risks than cutting too late.
